h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to continue rehearsing the same three-element checklist (top box, bottom box, bar line) until the task is complete.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: [sets the bar-line tile after the third beat space and takes the pen] 'Top box is 3, lower box is the quarter-note unit. The staff matches: 3 over 4 at the start, one bar line after beat three, speed mark outside the boxes. I'll ink 3 over 4.'
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically places the bar-line tile and picks up the pen to ink the signature, completing the procedural task, but this is the culmination of a single unvarying route rather than a new device or criterion.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure or breakdown; the tutor repeats and slightly consolidates the same checklist move across all turns without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; all tutor turns continue the pre-established lesson plan unchanged.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: [sets the bar-line tile after the third beat space and takes the pen] 'Top box is 3, lower box is the quarter-note unit. The staff matches: 3 over 4 at the start, one bar line after beat three, speed mark outside the boxes. I'll ink 3 over 4.'
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner physically places the bar-line tile and picks up the pen to ink the signature, completing the procedural task, but this is the culmination of a single unvarying route rather than a new device or criterion.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn, so no recognition-contingent tutor uptake is possible; all tutor turns continue the pre-established lesson plan unchanged.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure or breakdown; the tutor repeats and slightly consolidates the same checklist move across all turns without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure or breakdown; the tutor repeats and slightly consolidates the same checklist move across all turns without any stock-taking contrast or new mechanism.

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue methodically placing each label and arrow under its source stack as prompted by the tutor.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The friction arrow goes on the cart at the table contact, along the surface. If the push tends the cart right, friction points left. Is that the condition?
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner physically places the friction label at the table-contact stack and applies the correct directional criterion (opposite the slipping tendency, not opposite travel), performing a new representational device rather than merely accepting or repeating the tutor's instruction.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Use this stack as the key. Where does the friction arrow go on the cart if its source is table contact, not travel?
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor redirects from the learner's residual travel-arrow framing by foregrounding the source stack as the key, but this is a narrowing of the same route rather than a full stock-taking contrast plus a genuinely new mechanism, so it does not meet the 4–5 threshold.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Same source stack. Place the normal arrow at the table-contact edge. Which way should it point on the cart?
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor extends the source-stack device to the normal force after the learner correctly places friction, but this continues the established task structure rather than visibly adapting to a revised learner framing.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: The friction arrow goes on the cart at the table contact, along the surface. If the push tends the cart right, friction points left. Is that the condition?
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner physically places the friction label at the table-contact stack and applies the correct directional criterion (opposite the slipping tendency, not opposite travel), performing a new representational device rather than merely accepting or repeating the tutor's instruction.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: Same source stack. Place the normal arrow at the table-contact edge. Which way should it point on the cart?
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor extends the source-stack device to the normal force after the learner correctly places friction, but this continues the established task structure rather than visibly adapting to a revised learner framing.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Use this stack as the key. Where does the friction arrow go on the cart if its source is table contact, not travel?
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor redirects from the learner's residual travel-arrow framing by foregrounding the source stack as the key, but this is a narrowing of the same route rather than a full stock-taking contrast plus a genuinely new mechanism, so it does not meet the 4–5 threshold.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Use this stack as the key. Where does the friction arrow go on the cart if its source is table contact, not travel?
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor redirects from the learner's residual travel-arrow framing by foregrounding the source stack as the key, but this is a narrowing of the same route rather than a full stock-taking contrast plus a genuinely new mechanism, so it does not meet the 4–5 threshold.
